europeTrademark: Distributed Open Marketplace for Europe (DOME) Project has received funding from European Union’s Digital Europe Programme under the Grant Agreement No 101084071.

whatsdome._desc: DOME is the European marketplace that provides a unified environment where trust, compliance, and interoperability form the foundation of how digital services are published, compared, and used across Europe.

trusted.title: Trusted, Sovereign Digital Ecosystem
trusted.desc: DOME ensures services operate under EU rules for security, data protection, and sovereignty, creating a reliable environment for digital operations across Europe.

transparency.title: Transparency and Comparability
transparency.desc: Consistent documentation, pricing clarity, and shared trust indicators make it easy to understand, compare, and assess digital services.

interoperability.title: Interoperability and Federation
interoperability.desc: DOME connects multiple marketplaces and providers through shared standards, enabling seamless discovery, integration, and cross-market collaboration.

fairAccess.title: Fair and Open Market Access
fairAccess.desc: The ecosystem removes barriers to entry, supports SMEs, and prevents vendor lock-in, ensuring all participants can compete and grow on equal terms.

1.question: Am I eligible to become a DOME Marketplace provider?
1.answer:
To join the DOME Marketplace as a service provider, your organisation must meet the following criteria:
  • EU/EEA establishment: Your organisation must be a legally registered entity within the EU/EEA, or have a legal entity established in the EU/EEA (e.g., a subsidiary, branch, or other registered entity).
  • Service ownership: You must offer cloud, edge, or digital services that your organisation owns and operates, not acting solely as a reseller of third-party services.
  • B2B/B2G focus: Your target customers must be businesses, public organisations, or professionals, not individual consumers.

2.question: Are my services eligible to be listed on the DOME Marketplace?
2.answer:
Your services are eligible to be listed if they meet the mandatory compliance criteria defined by the DOME compliance framework.

DOME applies the following compliance profiles:
  • Baseline: Your offering can be listed with a Baseline status if it meets the mandatory criteria through self-attestation (self-assessed compliance), even if it does not have formal certifications.
  • Professional / Professional+: Your offering may qualify for Professional or Professional+ if it is supported by certified compliance evidence (valid certifications). The level achieved depends on the type and number of valid certifications, in line with DOME requirements.
  • Non-compliant: If your offering fails one or more mandatory compliance criteria—based on self-attestation and/or certification checks—it is classified as non-compliant and cannot be published in the official DOME catalogue.

Services published on DOME must meet at least the Baseline compliance requirements.

More information on the compliance profiles and their criteria can be found here.

3.question: Do I need to pay a fee to become a DOME Marketplace provider?
3.answer:
No. There is currently no upfront fee to join the DOME Marketplace.
DOME operates on a revenue-sharing model, meaning providers only pay a small fee when a service is successfully sold through the DOME marketplace.

Please note that DOME does not intervene in the commercial relationship or service delivery but only enables discovery, evaluation, and transactions between providers and customers.

You can find more information on the DOME pricing model here.

4.question: What information and documentation are required to onboard DOME as a provider?
4.answer:
To onboard as a provider, you will be asked to provide basic organisational details, nominate key roles, and submit supporting documentation for verification.

Required information
  • Company information: Legal name, VAT number, country, city, registered office address, postal code
  • Legal Representative information: Identification and contact details of the individual legally representing the company
  • Account Administrator information: Identification and contact details of the individual responsible for managing the organisation’s account on DOME (this can also be the same person as the Legal Representative)

Required documentation

You have to provide the Account Administrator Appointment form duly signed.

If the Appointment form is signed using an eIDAS for legal entity representatives certificate, nothing additional is required from your side. Otherwise, you have to additionally provide evidence of the existence of the company and the capacity to represent the company of the Legal Representative. Typically, this is achieved through the submission of a Certificate of Incorporation of the company, clearly indicating the legal representatives authorised to represent the company. If this information is not reflected in the incorporation document, an additional Power of Attorney (or equivalent document) must be provided. When the original documentation is not issued in English, you will have to submit an English translation of the said documents.

5.question: What is the process for becoming a DOME Marketplace provider?
5.answer:
The onboarding process is straightforward and can be completed in two steps:

Step 1 – Self-registration
You can quickly create a company account and access the platform. Once you receive your verifiable credentials, you can browse the marketplace, review existing offerings, prices and commercial models, even procure services while starting drafting your own service listings. This step allows you to explore and prepare, but it does not allow you to publish services or sell through the marketplace.

Step 2 – Full onboarding
To publish services and start selling through the DOME Marketplace, you must complete the full onboarding process by submitting the required company and role information, along with the supporting documentation described above. Once verification is completed and your onboarding is approved, your organisation can publish its services and start selling on the DOME Marketplace.

6.question: I am registered as a DOME marketplace provider. Can I also procure services?
6.answer: Yes. If you are registered as a provider on the DOME Marketplace, your organisation can also procure services as a customer. The same company account can be used to browse, request offers, and purchase services from other providers on the platform.

cta.title: Ready to grow your business across Europe?
cta.subtitle: Join DOME Marketplace — built for trusted digital service procurement in Europe

eligibility.question: Am I eligible to use the DOME Marketplace as a customer?
eligibility.answer:
The DOME Marketplace is designed for organisations or professionals worldwide looking to procure trusted digital services in a transparent environment.

You can use the DOME Marketplace if you represent:

  • Businesses (B2B) seeking cloud, edge, AI, or digital services
  • Public sector organisations (B2G) such as public administrations, agencies, or research institutions
  • Professional organisations purchasing digital services for operational use

Or you are an individual acting within his/her trade, business or professional activities. That is, an individual not acting as a consumer.

trustworthiness.question: How does DOME ensure the trustworthiness of the services listed on the Marketplace?
trustworthiness.answer:
Services listed on the DOME Marketplace are evaluated against the DOME compliance framework, which helps customers understand the level of compliance and supporting evidence behind each offering.

Each service is assigned a compliance profile, such as:

  • Baseline – The service meets the mandatory compliance requirements through provider self-attestation.
  • Professional / Professional+ – The service is supported by verified certifications and compliance evidence.

This structured approach allows customers to assess services more easily, compare offerings transparently, and select solutions that align with their compliance and governance requirements.

Services that do not meet at least the Baseline Compliance Criteria are not published in the DOME catalogue.

You can find more information on the DOME Compliance framework here

fee.question: Do I need to pay a fee to become a DOME Marketplace customer?
fee.answer:
No. There is no fee for organisations and professionals to join and use the DOME Marketplace as a customer.

Please note that DOME does not intervene in the commercial relationship or service delivery but only enables discovery, evaluation, and transactions between providers and customers.

process.question: What is the process for becoming a DOME Marketplace customer?
process.answer:
Customer onboarding is completed in a single step. You provide:

  • Company information (legal name, VAT, address, country)
  • Company Representative information (information of the person acting on behalf of the organisation)

Once submitted, you receive your verifiable credentials and gain full access to the DOME platform.

If you are a professional (freelancer):

You onboard as an organisation. In this case, the company/legal name should match your professional name (as registered for your professional activity).

provider.question: I am registered as a DOME marketplace customer. Can I also be a service provider?
provider.answer:
Yes. If you are already registered as a customer on the DOME Marketplace, you can also sell services. To do so, you simply need to complete the provider onboarding process.

Your existing company account remains the same. By completing the additional onboarding steps required for providers, such as submitting the necessary company information and documentation, you will gain the ability to publish and offer services on the marketplace.

Once the provider onboarding is completed and verified, your organisation will be able to both procure services and sell its own offerings on DOME. You can find more information here and onboard as a DOME marketplace provider.

dispute.question: What happens in case of a dispute with a service provider?
dispute.answer:
Contracts for services are established directly between the customer and the provider. Any dispute related to pricing, service delivery, or contractual terms is therefore handled between the contracting parties, in accordance with the terms of their agreement and the applicable law.

DOME facilitates discovery and engagement between customers and providers and is not a party to the service contract. As such, DOME does not adjudicate contractual disputes but may, where appropriate, facilitate communication between the parties.

ABOUT_DOME._subtitle: Your one-stop-shop for trusted Cloud and Edge services.
ABOUT_DOME._description: DOME Marketplace is a comprehensive online catalogue of cloud and edge offerings, available through a collection of independent marketplaces and different product and service providers.
ABOUT_DOME._description2: The uniqueness of the DOME marketplace is its ability to generate and use verifiable credentials ensuring the publication of trusted services, according to EU regulation. In other words, it enables a standardised process to verify that services and providers are certified and trustable.
ABOUT_DOME._benefits: Among its benefits, DOME Marketplace offers several advantages to users:
ABOUT_DOME._trusted: Provides trusted and standardised access to service providers’ offers, matching customers’ demands.
ABOUT_DOME._verified: Ensures verified and comparable qualifications across services and products.
ABOUT_DOME._transactions: Facilitates simple and transparent transactions and service management.
ABOUT_DOME._whydescription: DOME Marketplace, with this single aggregated catalogue of products delivered by pre-validated European suppliers, aims to streamline the procurement journey, improve vendor selection, reduce search and evaluation time, facilitate product comparison, accelerate purchasing processes and simplify spending analysis, benefiting both buyers and suppliers within the European ecosystem.
ABOUT_DOME._central: DOME Marketplace: A central piece of Europe’s Digital future.
ABOUT_DOME._aligned: DOME Marketplace is aligned with Europe’s vision of a digital future powered by cloud computing. The marketplace has been deployed by the DOME project, as part of the European Commission’s strategy to co-invest with Member States in the development of new digital infrastructures, as well as to improve existing capabilities at both national and EU levels.
ABOUT_DOME._singlepoint: The goal is to provide European companies and public organisations with the single-point access to trusted cloud and edge services, necessary for digital transformation. In this context, DOME Marketplace has been designed to offer a better and greater availability of trusted cloud and edge services, offered by providers and marketplaces members of the DOME ecosystem,
ABOUT_DOME._focus: focusing on increasing the adoption of these services among public and private entities, particularly in sectors that handle sensitive data, and who may be lagging behind in cloud adoption. This marketplace is a fundamental part of the EU’s objective to strengthen open strategic digital autonomy and boost Europe’s digital future.
ABOUT_DOME._explore: Explore the DOME project website if you want to know more!

CONTACTUS._purpose: Purpose: to send you information about DOME's project by electronic means. Legal basis: data subject's consent. CONTACTUS._indicating: indicating in the subject line: 'Exercise of rights'. You are also entitled to be assisted by a Data Protection Authority. Find more information about personal data processing in
